summary: Changed repository shell scripts from non-executable mode 100644 to executable mode 100755 so they can be run directly on Linux without permission complaints. The PR was a small tooling/permission fix with no source-code logic changes and no recorded review discussion.
what_changed: Set executable bit on build.sh, changing file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Set executable bit on gen.sh, changing file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Set executable bit on resync.sh, changing file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Set executable bit on tools/generate_context.sh, changing file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Set executable bit on tools/newlines.sh, changing file mode from 100644 to 100755.
No textual additions or deletions were recorded; the diff excerpt only shows file mode changes.
smart_moves: Fixed the scripts at the repository metadata level instead of requiring every Linux user to invoke them through `sh script.sh` or manually run chmod.
Applied the executable permission consistently to all known `*.sh` scripts in the shown diff rather than fixing only one entry point.
The PR body explicitly called out the Linux motivation and acknowledged possible Windows concerns, keeping the scope clear.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation repositories with build, generation, resync, or formatting helper scripts, executable permissions are part of the developer workflow and should be tracked in git.
Small repository-metadata fixes can remove cross-platform friction without touching matching-sensitive source code.
When adding new shell scripts, ensure they are committed with mode 100755 if they are intended to be run directly.
naming_conventions: Shell helper scripts use concise lowercase names with `.sh` suffix, such as `build.sh`, `gen.sh`, and `resync.sh`.
Tooling scripts under `tools/` also retain `.sh` suffixes, for example `tools/generate_context.sh` and `tools/newlines.sh`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were included in the PR context.
No reviewer-requested changes or naming feedback were present.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs adding new .sh scripts to confirm they preserve executable mode 100755 when intended as direct commands.
Search for build or CI issues caused by script permission errors on Linux runners.
Check whether Windows tooling ever required alternate handling for executable shell-script modes.
